2605.12918.title: CommonWhy: A Dataset for Evaluating Entity-Based Causal Commonsense Reasoning in Large Language Models
2605.12918.paper_summary: To effectively interact with the real world, Large Language Models (LLMs) require entity-based commonsense reasoning, a challenging task that necessitates integrating factual knowledge about specific entities with commonsense inference. Existing datasets for evaluating LLM entity-based commonsense reasoning have largely focused on True/False or multiple-choice questions, leaving the explicit assessment of the model's ability in abductive reasoning about causes and effects and generating explanations largely unexamined. In this work, we introduce CommonWhy, a dataset of 15,000 why questions designed to evaluate entity-based commonsense reasoning about causal relationships in LLMs. CommonWhy also serves as a Knowledge Graph Question Answering (KGQA) benchmark, as all supporting knowledge required to answer its queries is available in the Wikidata knowledge graph. Unlike existing KGQA datasets, which primarily test fact retrieval, CommonWhy targets causal commonsense reasoning, establishing a new paradigm for KGQA evaluation. Experiments with state-of-the-art LLMs and LLM-based KGQA methods reveal their significant shortcomings, including frequent factual hallucinations and failures in causal reasoning.
2605.12918.paper_summary_zh: 為了有效地與現實世界互動，大型語言模型（LLMs）需要基於實體的常識推理，這是一項挑戰性的任務，需要將有關特定實體的事實知識與常識推理相結合。現有的評估LLM基於實體的常識推理的數據集主要集中在真/假或多選題上，對模型在推理因果關係和生成解釋方面的能力的明確評估仍然未得到充分檢驗。在這項工作中，我們介紹了CommonWhy，這是一個包含15,000個「為什麼」問題的數據集，旨在評估LLM在因果關係方面的基於實體的常識推理。CommonWhy還作為知識圖譜問題回答（KGQA）基準，因為回答其查詢所需的所有支持知識都可以在Wikidata知識圖譜中獲得。與現有的KGQA數據集不同，後者主要測試事實檢索，CommonWhy則針對因果常識推理，建立了一種新的KGQA評估範式。對最先進的LLMs和基於LLM的KGQA方法的實驗揭示了它們的重大缺陷，包括頻繁的事實幻覺和因果推理的失敗。
2605.12918.author: Armin Toroghi et.al.
2605.12918.authors: Armin Toroghi, Faeze Moradi Kalarde, Scott Sanner
